title: Scientists hope to learn about alien intelligence from Frederick Sound humpback whales
summary: From left to right: Skipper Josef Quitslund, and Whale-SETI affiliates Fred Sharpe, Jack Mezzone, Rachel Meade, and Joe Olson throw up the Vulcan Salute at the Five Finger Lighthouse on July 24, 2024. (Photo by Shelby Herbert/KFSK) Researchers from an organization that seeks out extraterrestrial life camped out at a remote lighthouse in Southeast Alaska for the better part of the summer, but they weren’t out there looking for little green men. They were there to look for — and listen to — the humpback whales that swarm the waters of the Frederick Sound. KFSK’s Shelby Herbert hopped on a boat to learn more about what life beneath the waves could tell us about the life that could exist among the stars. I’m sailing tow...

title: Two friends are trying to make tanning hides a thing in Petersburg
summary: From left to right: Greta He aly, Anna Ziegler, Laura Holloway, and Kara Ziegler stretch a treated deer hide with help from baby Calvin on October 20, 2024. (Photo by Shelby Herbert/KFSK) Two self-described “amateur tanners” in Southeast are trying to build a community around turning animal hides into leather. KFSK’s Shelby Herbert got her hands dirty at their first workshop in Petersburg, and brings us this story. It’s a rare bluebird day in Petersburg, but locals aren’t the only ones out tanning in the sun. A group of students are handling deer and...

title: Alaska Native blood quantum clarified for who can hunt sea otters
summary: Kootink Heather Douville of Craig teaches workshop participants how to skin a sea otter at the Elders and Youth Conference in Anchorage, Oct. 15. The workshop was hosted by the Central Council of Tlingit and Haida Indian Tribes of Alaska. (Photo by Angela Denning/CoastAlaska) For years, the regulations about who could hunt sea otters in Alaska were confusing. Many thought hunters must be at least one-quarter Alaska Native and belong to a coastal tribe. But a recent opinion from the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service confirms that any coastal tribal mem...

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: November 14, 2024
summary: November 14, 2024: The Arctic sea ice has continued to increase this week but at a lower rate of 6.95% (as opposed to last week’s increase rate of 10.61%), from the level on 11/07/2024 of 8.438 M km 2 to 9.068 M km 2 for this week. title: The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of 11 November, 2024
summary: Take Five is The Arctic Institute's weekly redux on the Arctic's most important stories, published every Friday. The post The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of 11 November, 2024 appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
